title: STANDARD FOR QUINOA CXS 333-2019 Adopted in 2019. Amended in 2020.

sections.section: STANDARD FOR QUINOA CXS 333-2019 Adopted in 2019. Amended in 2020.
sections.summary: Document title with adoption and amendment dates (Standard for Quinoa CXS 333-2019; adopted 2019, amended 2020) and not a substantive regulatory provision.
Applies to processed quinoa (per Section 2.2) suitable for human consumption, packaged or in bulk, and explicitly excludes quinoa for propagation (seed) and derived products such as flour or flakes.

Defines 'quinoa' as the grain from Chenopodium quinoa Willd. and 'processed quinoa' as quinoa grain that has been cleaned, had the saponin-containing pericarp removed, and been sorted by color and size.
Defines quinoa as the grain obtained from the plant species Chenopodium quinoa Willd.
Defines processed quinoa as quinoa grain (Chenopodium quinoa Willd.) that has been cleaned, had the saponin-containing pericarp removed, and been sorted by color and size.

Sets essential composition and quality factors: general requirements that quinoa be safe, free of abnormal flavours/odours and living insects/mites, and that processed quinoa have characteristic color; and specific limits including maximum moisture 13.0% m/m, extraneous matter (organic and inorganic) 0.1% each, defect definitions with maximums (broken 3.0%, damaged 2.5%, germinated 0.5%, coated 0.3%, immature 0.9%), minimum protein 10.0% (dry basis), and maximum saponin 0.12%.

Lists general quality requirements for quinoa: it must be safe and suitable for human consumption, free from abnormal flavours and odours, free from living insects and mites, and processed quinoa should have a characteristic color (commonly white, black or red).

sections.section: 3.1.1 Quinoa shall be safe and suitable for human consumption.
sections.summary: Requirement that quinoa be safe and suitable for human consumption.

sections.section: 3.1.2 Quinoa shall be free from abnormal flavours and odours.
sections.summary: Requirement that quinoa be free from abnormal flavours and odours.

sections.section: 3.1.3 Quinoa shall be free from living insects and mites.
sections.summary: Requirement that quinoa be free from living insects and mites.

sections.section: 3.1.4 Processed Quinoa color should be characteristic, where the most common are white (pearly, pale, grayish), black and red, among others.
sections.summary: Stipulates that processed quinoa colour should be characteristic, with the most common colours being white (pearly, pale, grayish), black and red.

Specifies specific quality factors for quinoa, setting a maximum moisture (13.0% m/m), limits for extraneous matter (organic and inorganic each 0.1%), definitions and maximums for defective grains (broken, damaged, germinated, coated, immature), a minimum protein content (10.0% on a dry matter basis) and a maximum saponin content (0.12%).
Sets a maximum moisture content for quinoa of 13.0% m/m and notes that lower moisture limits may be required for specific destinations depending on climate, transport duration and storage conditions.

Defines extraneous matter for quinoa as all organic and inorganic materials other than quinoa and sets maximum limits of 0.1% for organic extraneous matter (e.g., husks, stem parts, animal-origin impurities, other seeds, leaves) and 0.1% for inorganic extraneous matter (e.g., stones).

sections.section: 3.2.2.1 Organic extraneous matter includes husks, stem parts, impurities of animal origin, other seed species, and leaves.
sections.summary: States that extraneous matter is any material other than quinoa and specifies organic (husks, stem parts, animal-origin impurities, other seed species, leaves) and inorganic (stones) categories, each with a 0.1% maximum.

sections.section: 3.2.2.1.1 Organic extraneous matter includes husks, stem parts, impurities of animal origin, other seed species, and leaves. 0.1% maximum.
sections.summary: Lists organic extraneous matter in quinoa (husks, stem parts, impurities of animal origin, other seed species, leaves) and limits it to a maximum of 0.1%.

sections.section: 3.2.2.1.2 Inorganic extraneous matter includes stones. 0.1% maximum.
sections.summary: Identifies inorganic extraneous matter in quinoa as stones and sets a maximum limit of 0.1%.

Defines defect categories for quinoa—Broken, Damaged, Germinated, Coated and Immature grains—by their distinguishing characteristics (size reduction from mechanical action; alteration by physical/chemical/biological agents; radicle development; retained shell or flower parts; and lack of physiological maturity with small greenish appearance, respectively).

Provides definitions for quinoa defects: Broken Grains (pieces < three quarters of whole grain from mechanical action), Damaged Grains (altered in form/structure by physical, chemical or biological agents), Germinated Grains (radicle/embryo development), Coated Grains (retain perigone or part of flower), and Immature Grains (not physiologically mature, small and greenish).
Specifies maximum allowable defective grain percentages for the product: Broken grains 3.0%, Damaged grains 2.5%, Germinated grains 0.5%, Coated grains 0.3%, and Immature grains 0.9%.
Requires a minimum protein content of 10.0% on a dry matter basis for the product.
Limits saponin content in the product to a maximum of 0.12%.
Prohibits the use of any food additives in the product.
Requires the product to comply with the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and with Codex-established maximum residue limits for pesticide residues.

Recommends that the product be prepared and handled according to the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC1-1969) and states it should comply with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.

States that quinoa shall be packaged in containers that safeguard its hygienic, nutritional, technological and organoleptic qualities, and that packaging must be safe, suitable, not transfer toxic materials/odours/flavours, and use food-grade, clean, new materials of adequate quality.
States the product must be labelled in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985).

Labeling requirement: the product name on the label must be either 'quinoa' or 'processed quinoa' as defined in Section 2, with optional additional information (e.g., origin, quality, color) permitted.

For non-retail containers, information may appear on the container or in accompanying documents, but the product name, lot identification, and manufacturer/packer name and address must appear on the container, and lot identification and manufacturer/packer details may be replaced by an identification mark if that mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.

Compliance with this Standard must be checked using the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) that are relevant to the Standard's provisions.
